Humanoid State Estimation ← back to dashboard

▍ Stage 0 — The Harness

real Asimov modelhead camera addedwalking = black boxground truth logged

Before any estimation, Stage 0 stands up the foundation: load the real robot, expose exactly the sensors it carries onboard, let it walk, and log the ground truth to grade every later stage against. Everything downstream only ever sees the onboard sensors — never the ground truth.

The robot, and where its sensors live
The actual Asimov mesh. Red/blue = original sensors, cyan = what we added, amber = derived. Drag the robot below to look around it.
drag the robot to rotate ↻ · or use the slider
● IMU● head camera● joint encoders● foot contact
original — ships with the model
  • IMU on the pelvis: a gyroscope (angular velocity) + an accelerometer (specific force)
  • Joint encoders: an angle at every joint (legs, waist, neck)
added / derived by us
  • Head camera (cyan): the robot’s eye — absent upstream; we bolted it on for the VIO
  • Foot contact (amber): not a sensor — there is no foot-force sensor on this model, so we derive “is this foot planted?” from the physics engine’s collision list
Onboard vs. eval-only — the honest split

The estimator is only allowed the estimator-visible stream. The ground truth is logged purely to score the estimate afterward and is never fed in — that is what keeps the evaluation honest.

estimator sees
  • raw gyro + accelerometer
  • 15 joint-encoder angles
  • foot-contact flags
  • head-camera frames
eval-only (ground truth)
  • true orientation
  • true base position + velocity
  • true world foot positions
Walking is a black box — and we say so

The model has zero actuators — on real hardware a learned policy supplies the joint torques. To produce motion for estimator development, we PD-track an open reference gait plus a torso “virtual support” that offloads ~44% of body weight and keeps it upright. This is a development stand-in, not true dynamic walking — but that is fine: estimation only needs the onboard sensor stream to be self-consistent with the motion, which it is.

TakeawayStage 0 delivers a clean, honest dataset: the exact onboard sensors a real Asimov carries, plus ground truth to grade against. What those raw signals actually are, and how they become a pose, is the IMU-101 primer.
